---
title: "MultiTaskingTrees"
author: "stellaRovazzi"
date: "2026-04-01"
output: html_document
---
Referenced the R documentation: https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/extraTrees/versions/1.0.5/topics/extraTrees .
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(extraTrees)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(tools)
library(tidyr)


folder_path <- "C:/Users/human/Documents/W26/Computational Intelligence/Project/awesomeGroupCI/RandomForest/RFInputs/"

file_list <- list.files(
  path = folder_path,
  pattern = "\\.csv$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

data_df <- map_dfr(file_list, function(file) {
  task_name <- substr(file_path_sans_ext(basename(file)), 1, 4)
  
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(task = task_name)
})

data_df <- data_df %>%  drop_na() %>%
    mutate(
      Date = as.Date(Date),
      task_id = as.integer(as.factor(task))
    ) %>%
    arrange(Date, task)

```

# Defining Paramaeters

```{r params}
# Lag columns expected in each CSV
lag_cols <- paste0("lag_", 1:60)

# Final holdout test size (by unique dates, not rows)
test_fraction <- 0.20

# Rolling CV settings on the training portion only
initial_train_days <- 60  
val_days <- 5             
step_days <- 20         

```

# Cleaning the data

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r cleaning}
required_cols <- c("Date", "Price", lag_cols, "task")
missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(data_df))

if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(
    paste(
      "The following required columns are missing:",
      paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
    )
  )
}

data_df <- data_df %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(Date),
    task = as.character(task)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(c("Date", "Price", lag_cols, "task"))) %>%
  arrange(Date, task)
```

```{r splitting_data}

all_dates <- sort(unique(data_df$Date))
n_dates <- length(all_dates)

if (n_dates < (initial_train_days + val_days + 10)) {
  stop("Not enough unique dates for the requested CV/test configuration.")
}

test_n_dates <- max(1, floor(n_dates * test_fraction))
test_start_idx <- n_dates - test_n_dates + 1

train_dates_full <- all_dates[1:(test_start_idx - 1)]
test_dates <- all_dates[test_start_idx:n_dates]

train_full <- data_df %>%
  filter(Date %in% train_dates_full)

test_data <- data_df %>%
  filter(Date %in% test_dates)

cat("Train date range:", min(train_full$Date), "to", max(train_full$Date), "\n")
cat("Test date range: ", min(test_data$Date), "to", max(test_data$Date), "\n")


```

```{r make_functions}
make_fixed_splits <- function(dates, train_days, val_days, step_days) {
  splits <- list()
  fold <- 1
  train_start_idx <- 1
  train_end_idx <- train_days

  while ((train_end_idx + val_days) <= length(dates)) {
    train_dates <- dates[train_start_idx:train_end_idx]
    val_dates <- dates[(train_end_idx + 1):(train_end_idx + val_days)]

    splits[[fold]] <- list(
      fold = fold,
      train_dates = train_dates,
      val_dates = val_dates,
      train_start = min(train_dates),
      train_end = max(train_dates),
      val_start = min(val_dates),
      val_end = max(val_dates)
    )

    train_start_idx <- train_start_idx + step_days
    train_end_idx <- train_end_idx + step_days
    fold <- fold + 1
  }

  splits
}

calc_metrics <- function(actual, predicted) {
  tibble(
    mae = mean(abs(actual - predicted)),
  )
}

```

```{r cross_validaiton}
cv_dates <- sort(unique(train_full$Date))

cv_splits <- make_fixed_splits(
  dates = cv_dates,
  train_days = initial_train_days,
  val_days = val_days,
  step_days = step_days
)

if (length(cv_splits) == 0) {
  stop("No CV folds were created. Reduce initial_train_days or val_days.")
}

cat("Number of CV folds:", length(cv_splits), "\n\n")


```


```{r cross-val_loop}
param_grid <- expand.grid(
  nodesize = c(3, 5, 10), # num of features left in terminal nodes
  numRandomCuts = c(2, 4, 8, 10), # good range
  mtry_frac = c(sqrt(60), 60/3), # will technically be 60 features
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# go through each set up in the parameter grid
all_cv_results <- purrr::pmap_dfr(param_grid, function(nodesize, numRandomCuts, mtry_frac) {

  # Train and validate the functions using rolling cross-fold
  fold_results <- map_dfr(cv_splits, function(s) {
    train_data <- train_full %>%
      filter(Date >= s$train_start & Date <= s$train_end)

    validation_data <- train_full %>%
      filter(Date >= s$val_start & Date <= s$val_end)

    seen_tasks <- unique(train_data$task_id)
    validation_data <- validation_data %>%
      filter(task_id %in% seen_tasks)

    if (nrow(validation_data) == 0) {
      return(tibble(
        fold = s$fold,
        mae = NA_real_,
      ))
    }

    x_train <- train_data %>%
      select(all_of(lag_cols)) %>%
      data.matrix()

    y_train <- as.numeric(train_data$Price)

    x_val <- validation_data %>%
      select(all_of(lag_cols)) %>%
      data.matrix()

    y_val <- as.numeric(validation_data$Price)

    p <- ncol(x_train)

    model <- extraTrees(
      x = x_train,
      y = y_train,
      nodesize = nodesize,
      mtry = mtry_frac,
      numRandomCuts = numRandomCuts,
      tasks = train_data$task_id
    )

    preds <- predict(model, x_val, newtasks=validation_data$task_id)
    metrics <- calc_metrics(y_val, preds)

    tibble(
      fold = s$fold,
      mae = metrics$mae,
    )
  })

  fold_results %>%
    mutate(
      nodesize = nodesize,
      numRandomCuts = numRandomCuts,
      mtry_frac = mtry_frac
    )
})

cv_summary_by_params <- all_cv_results %>%
  group_by(nodesize, numRandomCuts, mtry_frac) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_mae = mean(mae, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_mae = sd(mae, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_mae)

print(cv_summary_by_params)

best_params <- cv_summary_by_params %>%
  slice(1)

print(best_params)

```


```{r nodesize}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data = cv_summary_by_params, aes(x = as.factor(nodesize), y = mean_mae)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Multi-Task Node Size vs MAE",
       x = "Node Size",
       y = "Mean of MAE for Folds")
```
```{r nodesize}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data = cv_summary_by_params, aes(x = as.factor(numRandomCuts), y = mean_mae)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Multi-Task Number of Random Cuts vs MAE",
       x = "Random Cuts",
       y = "Mean of MAE for Folds")
```
```{r mtry}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(data = cv_summary_by_params, aes(x = as.factor(mtry_frac), y = mean_mae)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Multi-Task M try Fraction vs MAE",
       x = "M try",
       y = "Mean of MAE for Folds")
```

```{r evaluateithink}

nodesize = 3 # 5 and 3 are like basically equivalent
numRandomCuts = 10 # good range
mtry_frac = 20 # will technically be 60 features

x_train_full <- train_full %>%
  select(all_of(lag_cols)) %>%
  data.matrix()


y_train_full <- as.numeric(train_full$Price)

# Keep only tasks in test that were seen in train_full
seen_tasks_final <- unique(train_full$task_id)
test_data_eval <- test_data %>%
  filter(task_id %in% seen_tasks_final)

x_test <- test_data_eval %>%
  select(all_of(lag_cols)) %>%
  data.matrix()

y_test <- as.numeric(test_data_eval$Price)

p_final <- ncol(x_train_full)

final_model <- extraTrees(
  x = x_train_full,
  y = y_train_full,
  nodesize = nodesize,
  mtry = max(1, floor(p_final / 3)),
  numRandomCuts = numRandomCuts,
  tasks = train_full$task_id
)

test_preds <- predict(final_model, x_test, newtasks=test_data$task_id)

test_metrics <- calc_metrics(y_test, test_preds)

test_predictions <- test_data_eval %>%
  mutate(predicted_price = test_preds)
```


```{r}

# Playing around with the data so I can properly plot
# The previous metrics function wasn't really made for usage in a dataframe, so
# I just recalucalted it manually here
test_predictions <- test_predictions %>%
  mutate(
    error = Price - predicted_price,
    mae = abs(error)
  )

multi_results <- test_predictions %>%
  select(Date, Price, predicted_price, mae, task)

mean_multi_results <- test_predictions %>%
  group_by(task) %>%
  summarise(mae_mean = mean(mae, na.rm = TRUE))

print(mean_multi_results)
```


The code below was just modified for each plot I needed to call.
```{r plotting_results}

# grouping by month, so I can look at the seasonal prediction avcerages
month_summary <- test_predictions %>%
  filter(task == 'IBM_') %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(Date),
    month = as.integer(format(Date, "%m"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    actual_price = mean(Price, na.rm = TRUE),
    predicted_price = mean(predicted_price, na.rm = TRUE),
    error_var = var(error, na.rm = TRUE),
    error_sd = sd(error, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower = predicted_price - 1.96 * error_sd,
    upper = predicted_price + 1.96 * error_sd,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Saving the monthly labels
month_summary$month_label <- factor(
  month_summary$month,
  levels = 1:12,
  labels = month.abb
)

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(month_summary, aes(x = month_label)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group=1), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual_price, group=1), linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = predicted_price, group=1), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Multi-Tasking IBM Actual vs Predicted Price by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Price (Log(USD))"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


```
